subject: Public Health
title: HIV in Children
description: A short lesson to help you apply HIV in Children.

hook.text: 160,000 children were newly infected with HIV in 2021—almost all through mother-to-child transmission.

buildup.text: Without treatment, half of HIV-positive infants die before age two—the virus overwhelms immature immune systems.

discovery.text: Prevention of mother-to-child transmission—antiretrovirals during pregnancy and breastfeeding—reduces transmission.

twist.text: Only 52% of children living with HIV receive treatment—compared to 76% of adults—a persistent equity gap.

climax.text: Pediatric HIV formulations—child-friendly pills—are now available but supply chains still don't reach those in need.

punchline.text: No child should be born with HIV—we have the tools to prevent every case.

quiz.question: What percentage of children living with HIV receive treatment?
